name: La Rioja Alta Vineyards Circular
name_original: Badarán, Nájera y Ollauri — circular por los viñedos de la Rioja Alta
description: Cycle 42.1 miles through the vineyards of La Rioja Alta, passing charming towns like Badarán, Nájera, and Ollauri on this moderate route.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santo Domingo De La Calzada traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and the Oja River valley in La Rioja, Spain. The region features agricultural plains, vineyard views, and paths that integrate with the historic Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route. Cyclists will encounter varied terrain, from river crossings to areas with greater elevation, extending towards the Sierra de La Demanda. This diverse topography provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Santo Domingo De La Calzada The most popular road cycling route is Cruz de la Demanda and Valdezcaray Cycling Loop, a 67.0 miles (107.8 km) trail that takes about 6 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Rioja Alta Vineyards Circular, a moderate 42.1 miles (67.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views through the region's renowned vineyards and rolling countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Río Tirón – Puente de Cihuri loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail leading through the Oja River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Santo Domingo De La Calzada is defined by rolling hills, vineyard landscapes, and paths along the Oja River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Santo Domingo De La Calzada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santo Domingo De La Calzada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santo Domingo de la Calzada?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Santo Domingo de la Calzada, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the region offers several routes suitable for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1290749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tirón – Puente de Cihuri loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 52.6 km path through the Oja River valley. The Vía Verde del Oja (Oja Greenway) is also generally suitable for all fitness levels, often featuring mostly paved surfaces for an accessible experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this area?
A: Road cycling routes around Santo Domingo de la Calzada feature varied terrain. You'll encounter rolling hills, picturesque vineyard views, and paths along the Oja River valley. Some routes extend towards the Sierra de La Demanda, offering areas with greater elevation and more challenging climbs, while others traverse agricultural plains.

Q: Which routes offer challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990699946/cruz-de-la-demanda-and-valdezcaray-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Demanda and Valdezcaray Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 107.8 km trail with significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34327239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oja River Greenway – Alto de Pradilla loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 112.8 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the region's vineyards and the Oja River. You can also explore historic sites. The town itself features the 11th-century Santo Domingo de la Calzada Bridge and the Santo Domingo de la Calzada Cathedral. For natural beauty, consider routes that pass by highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock formations on the banks of the Tirón River&lt;/a&gt; or offer views from summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picota De San Torcuato&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34327239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oja River Greenway – Alto de Pradilla loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt; span over 112 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990699946/cruz-de-la-demanda-and-valdezcaray-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Demanda and Valdezcaray Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another extensive option at 107.8 km, providing a full day's ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santo Domingo de la Calzada?
A: The region's rolling hills and vineyard landscapes are particularly enjoyable in spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the scenery is vibrant. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures for cycling and showcase the natural beauty of La Rioja.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Santo Domingo de la Calzada are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990705646/la-rioja-alta-vineyards-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rioja Alta Vineyards Circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Restaurant La Estación – Llano de la Casa loop from Santo Domingo de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Santo Domingo de la Calzada?
A: The road cycling routes in Santo Domingo de la Calzada are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic vineyard views, and the integration with the historic Camino de Santiago, offering a rich cultural and physical experience.

Q: Are there any villages or towns I can visit on a road cycling tour?
A: Yes, the area is dotted with charming villages that can be incorporated into your cycling routes. Notable examples include Ezcaray and Grañón, which offer additional cultural and scenic stops, perfect for a break or to explore local life.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments to look out for?
A: Beyond the general landscape, you might encounter specific natural monuments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock formations on the banks of the Tirón River&lt;/a&gt; are a notable feature. The region also has beautiful tree formations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arboleda del Sur – Horse Chestnut Trees of Ezcaray&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Farm fields typical of this area of La Rioja. At certain times of the year, they are in vivid color with views of a large expanse of land.

Badarán is a municipality in Rioja Alta, in the Cárdenas River Valley and in what is called the Cañas Valley. Surrounded by the Sierra de la Demanda in the background, which gives spectacular views of the massif.

In Nájera they have been able to take advantage of the banks of the Najerilla River to make large green areas that cross the town and are filled with life in good weather.
There is a lawn, areas with benches, and the pilgrims do not hesitate to put their tired feet in the cold water.
If you need to make a stop in Nájera, this is a good place. In addition, in the town you will find shops, pharmacies and restaurants.
La Rioja is one of the Spanish provinces known for its vineyards and for what it represents. Along this road there are a large number of vineyards on both sides of the road.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can find parking options in the towns along the route, such as in Nájera. The highlight Park on the riverbank (Nájera) is a good reference point for parking near the river.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride for road cyclists. You'll experience rolling hills and valleys as you cycle through the picturesque La Rioja Alta region.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the quintessential landscapes of La Rioja Alta, winding through extensive vineyards. In the background, the majestic Sierra de la Demanda offers spectacular vistas. The journey also takes you through charming towns like Badarán, Nájera, and Ollauri.

faqs.question: Is this a challenging route for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'moderate' road ride. It covers about 67.7 kilometers with an elevation gain of 690 meters, requiring good fitness from cyclists. It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: How long does this racebike tour typically take?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete the route is around 3 hours and 1 minute, though this can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, connecting Badarán, Nájera, and Ollauri, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle through the vineyards of La Rioja Alta?
faqs.answer: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling in La Rioja Alta, with comfortable temperatures and beautiful vineyard colors. Summer can be quite hot, and winter may bring colder conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! You'll pass through the charming town of Badarán, see the beautiful Campos de Cañas, and cycle through the renowned Ollauri Vineyards. Nájera also offers a pleasant stop with its riverbank park.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas) and the Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior, among others.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route through the vineyards of La Rioja Alta.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces and through towns, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs. It's best enjoyed as a solo or group human cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns like Badarán, Nájera, and Ollauri, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and get water.
